arguments.command:
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try debuginfod-compatible endpoints
for URL in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" \
  "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" \
; do
  CODE=$(curl -sS -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$URL" --max-time 10)
  echo "$CODE -> $URL"
done

# Also try creating a minimal ELF stub with the BuildId, then using dotnet-symbol
python3 -c "
import struct

# Minimal ELF64 with a BuildId note
# ELF Header
e_ident = b'\x7fELF' + b'\x02\x01\x01\x00' + b'\x00'*8  # 64-bit, little-endian, Linux
ehdr = e_ident
ehdr += struct.pack('<HHIQQQIHHHHHH',
    3,  # e_type: ET_DYN (shared object)
    0xB7,  # e_machine: EM_AARCH64
    1,  # e_version
    0,  # e_entry
    64, # e_phoff (right after ELF header)
    0,  # e_shoff
    0,  # e_flags
    64, # e_ehsize
    56, # e_phentsize
    1,  # e_phnum
    64, # e_shentsize
    0,  # e_shnum
    0   # e_shstrndx
)

# Build ID note
buildid = bytes.fromhex('4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801')
note_name = b'GNU\x00'
note = struct.pack('<III', len(note_name), len(buildid), 3)  # NT_GNU_BUILD_ID = 3
note += note_name
note += buildid

# PT_NOTE program header
note_offset = 64 + 56  # after ehdr + phdr
phdr = struct.pack('<IIQQQQQQ',
    4,  # p_type: PT_NOTE
    0,  # p_flags
    note_offset,  # p_offset
    0,  # p_vaddr
    0,  # p_paddr
    len(note),  # p_filesz
    len(note),  # p_memsz
    4   # p_align
)

with open('/tmp/libcoreclr_stub.so', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(ehdr + phdr + note)

print('Created stub ELF with BuildId')
" 2>&1
